brief_title: Adalimumab Biosimilar Versus Originator in Moderate-to-Severe Rheumatoid Arthritis
official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-Blind Study Comparing the Efficacy, Safety, and Immunogenicity of ABP-501 (Adalimumab Biosimilar) to Reference Adalimumab in Patients with Moderate-to-Severe Rheumatoid Arthritis on Methotrexate
interventions: ABP-501 (adalimumab biosimilar) 40mg subcutaneous injection every 2 weeks
Reference adalimumab 40mg subcutaneous injection every 2 weeks
brief_summary: This Phase 3 study compares the efficacy, safety, and immunogenicity of ABP-501 (adalimumab biosimilar) to reference adalimumab in patients with moderate-to-severe active rheumatoid arthritis on stable methotrexate. The primary endpoint is ACR20 response at week 24.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Adults aged 18-80 years

2. Diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis per ACR/EULAR 2010 criteria for >= 6 months

3. Moderate-to-severe active disease: >= 4 swollen joints and >= 4 tender joints

4. DAS28-ESR >= 3.2 at screening

5. Positive for rheumatoid factor (RF) or anti-cyclic citrullinated peptide (anti-CCP) antibody

6. On stable methotrexate (7.5-25 mg/week) for >= 12 weeks prior to screening

7. Inadequate response to methotrexate monotherapy

8. No prior biologic DMARD therapy (biologic-naive)

9. Negative TB screening (QuantiFERON-TB Gold or tuberculin skin test)

10. Adequate hepatic function

11. Written informed consent

12. Able to self-administer subcutaneous injections or have a caregiver do so
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Prior treatment with any biologic DMARD or targeted synthetic DMARD

2. Active or latent tuberculosis

3. History of demyelinating disease (e.g., multiple sclerosis)

4. NYHA Class III-IV heart failure

5. Known malignancy within past 5 years (except non-melanoma skin cancer)

6. Active infection requiring IV antibiotics within 4 weeks or oral antibiotics within 2 weeks

7. Live or attenuated vaccine within 4 weeks of baseline

8. Known hypersensitivity to adalimumab or latex

9. Pregnant or breastfeeding

inclusion_criteria.text: Diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis per ACR/EULAR 2010 criteria for >= 6 months
Moderate-to-severe active disease: >= 4 swollen joints and >= 4 tender joints
Positive for rheumatoid factor (RF) or anti-cyclic citrullinated peptide (anti-CCP) antibody
On stable methotrexate (7.5-25 mg/week) for >= 12 weeks prior to screening
Negative TB screening (QuantiFERON-TB Gold or tuberculin skin test)
Able to self-administer subcutaneous injections or have a caregiver do so

exclusion_criteria.text: Prior treatment with any biologic DMARD or targeted synthetic DMARD
History of demyelinating disease (e.g., multiple sclerosis)
Known malignancy within past 5 years (except non-melanoma skin cancer)
Active infection requiring IV antibiotics within 4 weeks or oral antibiotics within 2 weeks
Live or attenuated vaccine within 4 weeks of baseline
Known hypersensitivity to adalimumab or latex
